hints: List indices start at 0, so the first element is items[0]. Negative indices count from the end: items[-1] is always the last element, no matter how long the list is.
To return two values as a tuple, wrap them in parentheses: return (items[0], items[-1]).
A slice like items[1:-1] gives you everything from the second element up to (but not including) the last element. That's the middle of the list.
If the list has exactly three elements, items[1:-1] returns a one-element list — just the single middle item. That's expected.
